stemHtml: While flying VFR "wings level" on top of clouds at 10 000 feet, your artificial horizon indicates a 20 degree bank.

options.a: You pull the circuit breaker to the artificial horizon knowing that perceptional illusions called “leans” might severely impair your control of the aircraft.
options.b: You trust the instrument and level off according to the instrument read-out.
options.c: You take the proper action by “leaning” toward the lower end of the artificial horizon, thus compensating for the perceptional illusion.
options.d: No attitude correction is needed since you know that your vision is more reliable than the instrument with regards to spatial orientation.
